steps.text: Stand tall next to {toy}. Reach for the ceiling like a tree growing.
Bend down and touch your toes — show {toy} how!
Twist side to side three times. {toy} watches and copies.
Hop on one foot for a count of five. Switch feet and hop again.
Take a deep breath. Tell {toy} you are ready for the day!
Gather three soft objects (pillows, plushies, towels) into one spot with {toy}.
Stack the objects to make a small tower or fort.
Place {toy} on top — or inside — of your build.
Decide what your build is for: a {slot} hideout, a lookout, a cozy nest?
Show your build to a grown-up and tell them the story of it.
Gather paper and crayons next to {toy}.
Draw {toy} as the main character of the picture.
Add a friend to the drawing — anyone or anything you choose.
Color in the background — the sky, the ground, or a room.
Hold up your drawing for {toy} to admire.
Sit cross-legged with {toy} resting on your lap.
Breathe in slowly through your nose for a count of four.
Hold the breath for two, then breathe out through your mouth for four.
Repeat the slow breath three more times — {toy} is breathing too.
Open your eyes. Whisper goodnight to {toy} or thank you for the calm.
{toy} announces a scavenger hunt for things related to {slot}.
Find one thing that is the right COLOR.
Find one thing that is the right SHAPE.
Find one thing that is the right SIZE.
Bring all three finds back to {toy} and tell the story they make together.
